# Test-Quality Review: `lib/features/sync/actor/` + `test/features/sync/actor/`

**Scope:** Four implementation files (1,839 non-generated lines) and four test files (4,252 lines).
**Generated by automated test-quality review (static analysis)**

---

## File inventory

| File | Lines | Has test? | Top issue |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lib/.../actor/sync_actor.dart` | 1,250 | Yes (`sync_actor_test.dart`, 2,633 lines) | OVERSIZED impl (>1000 lines); test also oversize |
| `lib/.../actor/outbound_queue.dart` | 198 | Yes (`outbound_queue_test.dart`, 781 lines) | Inline mock classes duplicate centralized mocks |
| `lib/.../actor/sync_actor_host.dart` | 138 | Yes (`sync_actor_host_test.dart`, 217 lines) | No fake-time control for the real-time `timeout: Duration(seconds: 30)` default |
| `lib/.../actor/verification_handler.dart` | 253 | Yes (`verification_handler_test.dart`, 621 lines) | `Timer.periodic` inside impl uses real wall-clock; test bypasses it via `pollInterval: Duration.zero` — valid but not via fakeAsync |

---

## File size / split opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `lib/features/sync/actor/sync_actor.dart` is **1,250 lines** — the single biggest oversized file in scope. It combines six distinct concerns that each have natural seams:
  1. State-machine dispatch (`handleCommand` + `_handleInit`, `_handleStop`, `_handleStartSync`, `_handleStopSync`) — ~lines 152–619
  2. Room/send commands (`_handleCreateRoom`, `_handleJoinRoom`, `_handleSendText`, `_handleKickOutbox`, `_handleConnectivityChanged`) — ~lines 621–764
  3. Verification commands (`_handleStartVerification` through `_handleGetVerificationState`, `_findUnverifiedPeerDevice`, `_refreshPeerDeviceKeys`, `_startVerification`) — ~lines 766–1007
  4. Outbox drain machinery (`_kickOutboxQueue`, `_drainOutboxQueue`, `_disposeOutboundQueue`, `_runWithRetries`, `_sendWithTransientSyncPause`) — ~lines 488–603
  5. Entrypoint (`syncActorEntrypoint`) and helper builders — ~lines 1188–1250
  6. Response helpers (`_ok`, `_error`, `_invalidState`, `_paramError`) — ~lines 1119–1186

  Recommended splits: extract `SyncActorVerificationMixin` / `SyncActorOutboxMixin` as `part` files or separate classes (each ~200–300 lines), and move the entrypoint to `sync_actor_entrypoint.dart`.
  **RESOLVED (adapted):** split via `part`-file extensions — `sync_actor_verification.dart` (282 lines), `sync_actor_room_commands.dart` (149 lines), and `sync_actor_outbox.dart` (122 lines); the handler class keeps the state-machine dispatch, init/stop, response helpers, and entrypoint at 715 lines. All moved members are private handlers dispatched from `handleCommand`, so no mock-delegators were needed.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/sync/actor/sync_actor_test.dart` is **2,633 lines** — exceeds ~500-line target by 5×. The file's own `createTestHandler` factory (lines 98–236) and Glados scenario infrastructure (lines 267–477) together constitute ~400 lines of shared scaffolding that could move to a `test/features/sync/actor/test_utils.dart`. Individual test groups (init, verification, stop, outbox drain, default-stream-factories) could split into separate files mirroring any impl-file split.
  **RESOLVED (assessed, no change):** the impl split used `part` files (one library), so the test stays the single mirror under the one-test-file-per-source rule; the `createTestHandler` factory and Glados scenario infrastructure already centralise the harness within the file.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/features/sync/actor/outbound_queue_test.dart` is **781 lines** — near the limit. The Glados scenario class and generator extension (lines 49–241) plus static helper functions (lines 243–324) form a coherent test-infrastructure block that could be extracted to `outbound_queue_test_utils.dart` to keep the test body under 500 lines.
  **RESOLVED (assessed, no change):** the generator/scenario block is cohesive test infrastructure used only by this file; moving it to a utils library would force its private types public and separate the property from its generators. 781 lines is under the working ceiling applied elsewhere in this effort.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/features/sync/actor/verification_handler_test.dart` is **621 lines** — borderline. The Glados generated types + generator extension (lines 9–227) is self-contained and could be extracted if the file grows further.

---

## Test quality improvements

- [x] **[HIGH]** **Inline mock classes duplicate centralized mocks.** `outbound_queue_test.dart:17-21` declares `MockMatrixSdkGateway`, `MockClient`, and `MockStateEvent` inline. `sync_actor_test.dart:29-50` declares `MockClient`, `MockMatrixSdkGateway`, `MockDeviceKeysList`, `MockDeviceKeys`, `MockKeyVerification`, `MockSyncUpdate`, `MockEncryption`, `MockCrossSigning`, `MockKeyVerificationManager` inline. `verification_handler_test.dart:7` declares `MockKeyVerification` inline. Per project rules, these must come from `test/mocks/mocks.dart`. `MockRoom` and `MockEvent` already exist there (lines 194, 198). The actor-specific types (`MockMatrixSdkGateway`, `MockClient`, `MockKeyVerification`, etc.) should be added to the centralized file and the inline declarations removed. **RESOLVED:** all nine inline mocks centralized (MockMatrixSdkGateway, MockSyncUpdate, MockEncryption, MockCrossSigning, MockKeyVerificationManager, MockStrippedStateEvent added to mocks.dart; MockClient → central MockMatrixClient; MockKeyVerification/MockDeviceKeys(.List) already central).

- [x] **[HIGH]** **Six uses of `await Future<void>.delayed(Duration.zero)` in `sync_actor_test.dart`** (lines 946, 2091, 2117, 2206, 2232, 2266) are fake-time policy violations. The policy forbids `Future.delayed()` in tests; these tests are not under `fakeAsync`, so they rely on real microtask scheduling. Each should be replaced with `await Future<void>.value()` (a zero-allocation microtask yield) or restructured to use a `Completer`-based completion signal, consistent with how the same file handles `verificationSeen`, `toDeviceSeen`, and `incomingMessageSeen` completers elsewhere. **RESOLVED:** all six replaced with `pumpEventQueue()` (no timers, full microtask drain).

- [x] **[MED]** **Verification test setup is copy-pasted across many tests in `sync_actor_test.dart`.** At least eight tests in the `verification` group (lines ~1716, ~1778, ~1849, ~1902, ~1944, ~2072, ~2098, ~2124) share the same pattern: declare `MockDeviceKeysList`, `MockDeviceKeys`, `MockKeyVerification`, stub their properties identically, then call `createTestHandler(onGatewayCreated: ...)`. The AGENTS.md rule "extract mock stub helpers when the same `when(...)` stub appears in 3+ tests" directly applies. Extract a `_buildVerificationHandler({required MockKeyVerification verification, ...})` helper and a `_stubDeviceKeys(MockClient client, {required DeviceKeys remote})` helper.
  **RESOLVED:** extracted `stubRemoteDevice` (10 folds), `stubVerificationReads` (3 folds), and `stubClientDeviceKeys` (5 folds) file-level helpers; the remaining per-test stubs (verificationMethods, SAS wiring, peer userIds) genuinely vary.

- [x] **[MED]** **`backfillStatsController` test boilerplate in `sync_actor_test.dart` outbox drain section** — `createTestHandler` call with the scripted-queue factory at lines 2580–2610 is 30 lines of inline factory setup. Since the actor test already defines `_ScriptedOutboundQueue`, consider extracting `_createHandlerWithScriptedQueue(List<Duration?> delays)` to reduce per-test noise.
  **RESOLVED (assessed, no change):** the scripted-queue factory appears exactly once in the file (the other grep hit is the createTestHandler parameter pass-through); a helper for a single call site adds indirection without removing duplication.

- [ ] **[LOW]** **`sync_actor_host_test.dart` uses a real 100 ms / 2 s `timeout` on Completers** (lines 117–124, 158–165, 187–191). These are real wall-clock waits on CI. The timeouts are essential for the test intent (verifying actual timeout and event delivery), but the `timeout: Duration(milliseconds: 100)` on the "slow command" path should be documented with a comment explaining why real time is acceptable here (it is a genuine isolate interaction, not a fake-time candidate).

- [ ] **[LOW]** **`test/features/sync/actor/outbound_queue_test.dart` does not use `setUpTestGetIt()` / `tearDownTestGetIt()`** — it creates `SyncDatabase(inMemoryDatabase: true)` directly, which is fine for this pure unit test. However, the tearDown only closes `db` (line 348) and does not guard against `getIt` state leaking into the broader test suite. This is acceptable since no `getIt` calls appear in `OutboundQueue`, but it should be documented.

---

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

The three existing Glados tests in this directory are well-designed and cover the key pure-logic surfaces:

- `outbound_queue_test.dart:652-779` — drain-scenario property (numRuns: 180, tagged)
- `sync_actor_test.dart:509-559` — command-sequence / state-machine property (numRuns: 120, tagged)
- `verification_handler_test.dart:264-314` — verification observation property (numRuns: 180, tagged)

- [x] **[MED]** **`_handleGetHealth` response shape invariant is not covered by Glados.** The property "getHealth always returns `ok: true` and the `state` field always equals `handler.state.name` regardless of actor state" is a pure, trivially expressible invariant that the current Glados state-machine test does not verify (it only checks the `state` field for `getHealth` commands at line 545). A small addition to the existing scenario model's `expectation` for `getHealth` to also capture the health map and assert `state == handler.state.name` after every command would add meaningful coverage without a new Glados test.
  **RESOLVED:** the Glados scenario's getHealth branch now asserts `ok == true`, `state == modeledState.name`, AND `state == handler.state.name` after every generated getHealth in every actor state.

- [x] **[MED]** **`_runWithRetries` backoff logic is a pure function with no Glados property test.** The retry loop at `sync_actor.dart:514-538` has clear invariants: (a) it makes exactly `min(attempt-of-first-success + 1, maxRetries)` attempts, (b) it throws on non-retryable errors without retry, (c) it backs off exponentially. A Glados property on a pure extracted helper (e.g. attempt count + success-on-attempt index + retryable flag → expectedAttempts, expectedResult) would be more robust than the two static tests at lines 1380-1452.
  **RESOLVED:** exposed via the `debugRunWithRetries` seam; a Glados3 property over (success-on-attempt, maxRetries, retryable) asserts the attempt-count/outcome oracle — non-retryable throws after exactly 1 attempt, retryable retries to success or exhausts at maxRetries.

- [ ] **[LOW]** **`_resolveSyncRoomId` in `OutboundQueue` has no property-based variant for room-list shapes beyond the six hand-crafted cases.** The existing static tests cover `explicit`, `uniqueMarked`, `noMarked`, `multipleMarked`, and `throwingAndUniqueMarked`, but the Glados drain-scenario only exercises room-mode as a parameter to `drain()`. A Glados property directly on `_resolveSyncRoomId` (stubbing different room-list topologies) would verify the selection invariant: "returns `roomId` iff exactly one room returns non-null from `getState`". Note: this is somewhat covered by the existing `outboundDrainScenario`; flag as low priority.

---

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [x] **[HIGH]** **`syncActorEntrypoint` cleanup path when `commandPort` receives a non-Map message is untested.** The entrypoint at `sync_actor.dart:1206-1210` silently returns if `raw is! Map`. `sync_actor_test.dart` tests `syncActorEntrypoint` but only exercises the `command is not String` and `missing command field` cases (lines 1110-1183) — not the case where the raw message is not a Map at all. This branch runs in the isolate, but the test already uses `syncActorEntrypoint` in-process (not via isolate), so a direct test is feasible. **RESOLVED:** new test sends a non-Map payload, asserts no crash/reply, and proves the loop still serves a follow-up stop command.

- [x] **[HIGH]** **`_pauseSyncLoopForSend` / `_sendWithTransientSyncPause` sync-pause mechanics are not directly tested.** These functions (sync_actor.dart:479-504) are exercised only incidentally via `sendText` while syncing. There is no test that verifies `abortSync` is called when `sendText` is issued in `SyncActorState.syncing` state. The test at lines 1314-1349 only exercises the `idle` path (explicitly calling `stopSync` first) and the `syncing` path without checking `abortSync` was called. **RESOLVED:** new sendText-while-syncing test verifies abortSync was called exactly once, backgroundSync toggled off/on, and the actor remains syncing afterwards.

- [x] **[MED]** **`_handleStop` cleanup error path (`stop: cleanup error (continuing)`) is only partially tested.** `sync_actor_test.dart:2317-2333` stubs `gateway.dispose()` to throw and confirms the command returns `ok: true`. However, it does not verify that `_syncSub`, `_loginStateSub`, etc. are all set to null after the error — i.e., that the `finally` block ran completely. Adding assertions on subsequent `getHealth` output (no loginState, state = `disposed`) would make this more meaningful.
  **RESOLVED (adapted):** the test now asserts post-stop getHealth — disposed state, gateway-derived fields reset (deviceId/userId null, encryption off, sync loop inactive). Note: `_latestLoginState` is a cached observation that stop deliberately does NOT clear (the review assumed it would) — documented in the test.

- [x] **[MED]** **`VerificationHandler._pollAll` timer-driven path is not tested with a real timer.** All `VerificationHandler` tests use `pollInterval: Duration.zero` or `pollInterval: const Duration(days: 1)`, which bypasses the `Timer.periodic` path entirely. The "timer fires and causes emission when state changed between poll ticks" flow is not covered. A `fakeAsync` test with `async.elapse(handler.pollInterval)` would cover the periodic poll branch without real wall-clock delay.
  **RESOLVED:** new fakeAsync test elapses the poll interval twice — no emission when nothing changed, then an emission when the step mutates WITHOUT firing onUpdate, which only the Timer.periodic branch can observe.

- [ ] **[LOW]** **`SyncActorHost.send` payload merging is not explicitly tested.** The `send` method merges `payload` into the command (line 87–91). There is no test that a non-trivial payload (e.g. `{'key': 'value'}`) is forwarded correctly and survives the SendPort round-trip. The existing test only sends `{'eventPort': ...}` and `{'data': ...}`.

---

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** **Six `await Future<void>.delayed(Duration.zero)` calls in `sync_actor_test.dart`** (lines 946, 2091, 2117, 2206, 2232, 2266) use the real event loop instead of `async.flushMicrotasks()`. In the very_good batched CI run these are fine individually, but they prevent converting those tests to `fakeAsync`, which would make them 10-100× faster and deterministic. Each resolves in one microtask under `fakeAsync` with `async.flushMicrotasks()`. **RESOLVED:** see line 43 — the calls are gone (pumpEventQueue); the fakeAsync conversion remains optional since the tests are already timer-free.

- [x] **[MED]** **`sync_actor_host_test.dart` uses real wall-clock timeouts** (100 ms and 2 s) for isolate round-trips. These tests cannot use `fakeAsync` because they cross an isolate boundary, but the 2 s `Completer.future.timeout` on event delivery (lines 158-165, 187-191) adds 2 s of worst-case CI latency per test if the isolate is slow. Consider reducing to 500 ms with a comment explaining the budget.
  **RESOLVED:** the 2 s event-delivery budget reduced to 500 ms with a comment (isolate round-trips resolve in milliseconds; the timeout is hang-failure headroom).

- [x] **[MED]** **`sync_actor_test.dart` Glados property at numRuns: 120 uses `Directory.systemTemp.createTempSync` in each iteration** (line 517). Each of the 120 Glados runs creates, uses, and deletes a real filesystem directory. This I/O adds measurable latency per run. The handler uses `useDefaultSyncDatabaseFactory: false` (in-memory DB), so the directory is only needed for the `init` command's `dbRootPath` parameter. Since the factory is overridden, passing a `/tmp/sync_actor_generated` constant path (without creating the directory) may work just as well and avoid 120 `createTempSync` / `deleteSync` round-trips.
  **RESOLVED:** the property now passes a constant `dbRootPath` (the in-memory DB factory override means the path is never touched on disk), eliminating 120 createTempSync/deleteSync round-trips.

- [ ] **[LOW]** **`verification_handler_test.dart` Glados at numRuns: 180** exercises `handler.dispose()` in every run (line 309), which cancels a `Timer.periodic`. Each iteration allocates a `VerificationHandler` with a `Duration(days: 1)` poll timer that is cancelled immediately. The overhead is negligible per run, but can be eliminated by using `addTearDown(handler.dispose)` outside the Glados loop (not feasible here since the handler is created inside the loop). No action needed.

---

## Summary

- **1 oversized implementation file** (`sync_actor.dart` at 1,250 lines — >250% over target); **1 oversized test file** (`sync_actor_test.dart` at 2,633 lines — 5× over target); `outbound_queue_test.dart` is borderline at 781 lines.
- **6 fake-time policy violations** (`await Future<void>.delayed(Duration.zero)` in `sync_actor_test.dart`) that should be `async.flushMicrotasks()` under `fakeAsync` or `await Future<void>.value()`.
- **~10 inline mock class declarations** across 3 test files that should be moved to or already exist in `test/mocks/mocks.dart`.
- **0 new Glados tests strictly required** — the three existing Glados tests cover the most valuable pure-logic surfaces; 2 MED-priority additions (`getHealth` invariant extension, `_runWithRetries` property) would meaningfully improve coverage of remaining pure logic.
- **Biggest opportunity:** split `sync_actor.dart` (1,250 lines) into 3–4 focused files (verification mixin, outbox mixin, entrypoint, core state machine) and correspondingly split `sync_actor_test.dart` — this would bring both files into guideline range and make the test suite far easier to navigate and maintain.
